message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Tasks 5-6 done, proceed with Task 7">
Status update:

**Tasks 5 and 6 are COMPLETE.** All a11y fixes applied, CI green on `6c757bc`.

**Task 7 (View todo list + Add todo) is now the active task.** I see some of you have already started domain types for todos (todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, etc.) in the working tree. That's the right next task — just make sure you follow the commit discipline:

1. Get to green → commit → push → verify CI
2. Do NOT accumulate uncommitted work
3. One thing at a time

**Commit discipline rules**: We have 8/9 consents (everyone except Carson and Luca). That's well past consensus. **Kent: please add the Commit Discipline section to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and commit it now.** Carson and Luca can register concerns after the fact if they have any.

**Carson, Luca**: Your consent votes are still welcome but we're proceeding with adoption.

Let's go. Task 7 — view todo list and add a todo item.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Tasks 5 and 6 are done. Task 7 (view todo list + add todo) is the active task. This is the core of the whole app — the screen users will spend 95% of their time on.

Let me review what's been built so far and check if the registration a11y fixes were applied.

content.text:
Good — the a11y fixes are in:
- Error elements always in DOM with `role="alert"` (line 26, 40, 53) — fixed
- Password hint "At least 8 characters" visible and linked via `aria-describedby` (line 36, 39) — fixed
- `minlength="8"` removed — fixed
- `aria-describedby` always present, `aria-invalid` conditional — correct pattern

The registration form is now solid from a UX+A11y standpoint. Now let me check the login template and then focus on Task 7.

content.text:
Auth forms are done and look good. Login is clean: "Sign in" button, "Don't have an account? Create account" link, `autocomplete="current-password"`, generic error via `role="alert"`. Missing the "Forgot password?" placeholder link I flagged earlier, but that's a minor item that can be added later.

Now — Task 7 is the big one. The todo list page. Let me proactively share the UX requirements with the team so they're building it right from the start.

input.content:
Luca, Task 7 (view todo list + add todo) is the core of the app. Here are the UX requirements — please have these in mind as you build it.

**The todo list page layout:**

```
[Header with nav — logout link]

My Todos                              (h1)

[Add todo input — always visible, full width]
  Label: "New todo"
  Placeholder: "What do you need to do?"

[Todo list — or empty state]
```

**Key UX requirements:**

1. **The add input is the hero of the page.** It sits above the list, always visible, full width. It's the most prominent interactive element. A visible `<label>` ("New todo") and placeholder text ("What do you need to do?").

2. **Empty state:** When the list is empty, show "No todos yet" in muted text below the input. The input field itself is the invitation to act — no tutorial, no illustration.

3. **After adding a todo:** The input clears and keeps focus. Ready for the next item. The new todo appears in the list. With HTMX: no page reload, the list updates in place. Without JS: full page reload, same result.

4. **Empty/whitespace submissions are silently ignored.** No error message for accidental Enter. Just do nothing.

5. **Todo items in a semantic `<ul>`.** Each item is an `<li>` with:
   ```
   [checkbox] Todo title text ............... [pencil] [X]
   ```
   (Edit and delete buttons come in Tasks 9 and 20 — for now, just checkbox + title.)

6. **Completed items:** Strikethrough + muted color (gray-400). Stay in place — the list never rearranges itself.

7. **The checkbox is a real `<input type="checkbox">` with a `<label>`.** Not a fake div. The label's `for` attribute matches the checkbox's `id`. Screen readers announce "Buy milk, checkbox, not checked."

8. **Aria-live announcements:** After adding a todo, the announcer region says "Added: Buy milk" (including the item title for context).

9. **Page title:** "My Todos -- Todo List" in the `<title>` element.

10. **Nav should include the logout link.** The user should always know how to leave.

The error copy for add-todo validation (title too long): "Todo title is too long (maximum 300 characters)". Only shown for titles exceeding max length — empty submissions are silent.

This is the screen the user will live on. Keep it simple, keep it fast, keep it obvious.
input.summary: Task 7 UX requirements for todo list page

content.text:
Task 7 is in progress. I've sent Luca the complete UX requirements for the todo list page. Registration and login are done and looking good — the a11y fixes were applied correctly.

This is the most important screen in the app. I'll be reviewing the todo list template as soon as it appears. Standing by.
